notes: Test prompts that exercise the skill across complexity, ambiguity, and domain breadth. Each prompt should force the skill to do real research, not pattern-match. Assertions are split into objective (programmatically checkable) and qualitative (human review).

evals.name: real-time-collaborative-editor
evals.prompt: I want to build a real-time collaborative document editor for my SaaS. Users should be able to edit the same doc simultaneously, see each other's cursors, and have it work offline. We're a small team and want to ship in 8 weeks. Stack is Node + Postgres on AWS.
evals.expected_behavior: Should ask context questions (Phase 1), decompose into conflict resolution / presence / persistence / transport (Phase 2), choose Deep mode (multiple sub-problems, correctness implied), search T1 (Figma, Linear, Liveblocks, Convex), recommend Yjs or Automerge with concrete picks, hand off to spec.

assertions.name: asks_context_questions
assertions.check: Output contains 4+ of: scale, stack, constraints, existing, priorities

assertions.name: decomposes_into_subproblems
assertions.check: Phase 2 lists at least 3 sub-problems

assertions.name: cites_real_sources
assertions.check: References at least 3 specific company engineering blogs (Figma/Linear/Liveblocks/Convex/Automerge/Yjs)

assertions.name: makes_recommendation
assertions.check: Has a clear 'Recommendation' section with a specific pick, not a survey

assertions.name: names_tradeoffs
assertions.check: Explicitly states what user is giving up

assertions.name: handoff_lines_present
assertions.check: Output ends with HANDOFF: lines

assertions.name: recommendation_matches_scale
assertions.check: Does NOT recommend FAANG-scale solution for small-team 8-week timeline

assertions.name: actionable_decisions
assertions.check: Decisions section has 3-6 concrete decisions

evals.name: multi-tenant-saas-billing
evals.prompt: Build out usage-based billing for my B2B SaaS. Customers pay per API call with metered tiers. Need accurate metering, near-real-time dashboards, and reconciliation with Stripe. Currently 50 customers, projecting 5000 in 18 months.
evals.expected_behavior: Should decompose into metering / aggregation / Stripe integration / tenant isolation. Should heavily reference Stripe Engineering, Modern Treasury, Increase. Should recommend event-sourced ledger + periodic aggregation + Stripe Metering integration.

assertions.name: stripe_engineering_referenced
assertions.check: References Stripe Engineering content

assertions.name: ledger_pattern_discussed
assertions.check: Mentions event sourcing or ledger pattern

assertions.name: tenant_isolation_addressed
assertions.check: Sub-problem or decision around tenant isolation

assertions.name: scale_appropriate
assertions.check: Recommendation appropriate for 50→5000 customers, not 5M

evals.name: vector-rag-pipeline
evals.prompt: Build a RAG system over our internal company docs (Confluence + Google Drive + Slack export). About 100k documents. Need accurate retrieval, source citations, freshness within a day. Team has Python/AWS experience.
evals.expected_behavior: Should reference BeanBot internal precedent (Tier 0), Anthropic engineering posts, Notion RAG writeup, hybrid search patterns. Recommend pgvector or Pinecone, hybrid retrieval (BM25 + vector), reciprocal rank fusion, document-level metadata.

assertions.name: checks_internal_precedent
assertions.check: Mentions BeanBot or internal repos as Tier 0 source

assertions.name: hybrid_retrieval_discussed
assertions.check: Mentions hybrid search, BM25+vector, or RRF

assertions.name: freshness_addressed
assertions.check: Addresses the daily-freshness requirement explicitly

assertions.name: leverages_user_experience
assertions.check: Builds on user's existing Python/AWS experience

evals.name: background-jobs-django
evals.prompt: I need background job processing for my Django app on Heroku. Want to avoid Redis if possible. ~50 jobs per day, mostly emails and report generation.
evals.expected_behavior: QUICK mode should activate (low scale, simplicity priority, single sub-problem). Should recommend procrastinate or django-q2 over Celery. Should NOT do Deep mode.

assertions.name: quick_mode_selected
assertions.check: Output indicates Quick mode chosen

assertions.name: no_overkill_recommendation
assertions.check: Does NOT recommend Celery+RabbitMQ or Kafka-based solution

assertions.name: specific_library_named
assertions.check: Names a specific Python library (procrastinate, django-q2, dramatiq, or RQ)

evals.name: vague-greenfield-startup
evals.prompt: I'm starting a new B2B SaaS. Want to build a 'workspace' product like Linear/Notion. Help.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill should refuse to research until Phase 1 produces a concrete answer. Should ask what specifically is being built (Linear-like = sync engine + issue model; Notion-like = block storage + collaborative editing — totally different problems).

assertions.name: asks_clarification
assertions.check: Skill asks the user to narrow scope before researching

assertions.name: good_clarifying_questions
assertions.check: Questions distinguish Linear-vs-Notion architecturally

evals.name: agentic-system-financial
evals.prompt: We want to build an agentic system at Fannie Mae that answers internal loan portfolio questions in natural language. Currently using direct SQL through a chatbot interface. Need to evolve to multi-step reasoning, tool use, multi-source retrieval (Redshift + S3 + internal docs).
evals.expected_behavior: Should pull BeanBot heavily as Tier 0. Should reference Anthropic agentic patterns, Uber QueryGPT, LinkedIn SQL Bot. Recommend LangGraph or similar orchestration, tool-use for SQL execution, intent routing, prompt caching for schema context.

assertions.name: internal_precedent_first
assertions.check: BeanBot mentioned as Tier 0

assertions.name: industry_references
assertions.check: References at least 2 of: Uber QueryGPT, LinkedIn SQL Bot, Anthropic agentic patterns

assertions.name: orchestration_addressed
assertions.check: Mentions LangGraph, agent orchestration, or state machine

assertions.name: respects_existing_arch
assertions.check: Recommendation evolves BeanBot rather than replacing it wholesale

evals.name: trivial-CRUD-should-NOT-trigger
evals.prompt: I need a REST endpoint that returns the current user's profile.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill should NOT trigger or, if invoked directly, should note that this doesn't warrant prior-art research and decline.

assertions.name: declines_or_acknowledges_overkill
assertions.check: Either skips or notes that this is trivial and doesn't need full research

evals.name: edge-case-feature-flags
evals.prompt: Implement feature flags for our growing SaaS. We're 200 engineers across 30 teams, need targeting rules, percentage rollouts, kill switches, and ideally A/B test integration.
evals.expected_behavior: Should reference LaunchDarkly, Statsig, Eppo, OpenFeature, and likely Optimizely. Should discuss build vs buy explicitly given team size. Should evaluate at least one in-house pattern (Netflix, Spotify, Shopify writeups).

assertions.name: discusses_build_vs_buy
assertions.check: Explicitly considers build-vs-buy tradeoff

assertions.name: names_concrete_vendors
assertions.check: Names at least 3 specific vendors or in-house systems

assertions.name: team_size_factors_in
assertions.check: 200-engineer team size affects the recommendation
